title: ACID RESISTANCE OF FLY-ASH –GGBS BASED GEOPOLYMER CONCRETE USING FIBRE
author_string: Rajasekhar, Sri S., Srinivas, Prof. B. Shesha
journal: Futuristic Trends in Mechanical Engineering Volume 3 Book 5
publisher: Iterative International Publishers, Selfypage Developers Pvt Ltd
abstract: Today, concrete is the second most used after water for global utilization. As concrete is formed by mixture of many chemicals which leads to many global changes in environment. Instead of cement, the waste produced from the steel and thermal industries such as ggbs, fly-ash are been replaced by cement. Such concrete is known as geopolymer concrete. This research is about study of durability properties of ggbs, fly-ash based geopolymer using fibers. Since geopolymer concrete cannot be direct mixable with water so here we have introduced water glass (sodium silicate). Water and water glass are taken in the ratio 0.3 by adding glass, polypropylene and steel fibers in 0.1%, 0.3%, 0.5% & 1.00%. These concretes are later performed durability studies acid & sulphate resistance test 7% for 28 days. The good results of highest compressive strength were obtained at 0.5% of glass fiber, 0.5% of polypropylene fiber & 1% of steel fiber.
